reviews.review_text:
Small town, so close to all services for location as opposed to others that are usually out of town 👍🏼
Ammenities clean and in good condition for their age 👍🏼
Park management and staff very amenable and friendly 👍🏼
After having left the Flinders over 20 years ago, it was nice to revisit the area at a sedate pace and kick back after a day of travel.
We traveled at a good time, weeks after the rains and enjoyed the Kaye’s back pace of the park 👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼

A wonderful quiet park with large gravel caravan sites. Clean and tidy amenities. Staff are friendly and willing to assist as required. Highly recommend this as your staying spot for all things Flinders Ranges.
Lovely Caravan Park if you want to set yourself a base camp for the Flinders Ranges.  Amenities clean and tidy and management were nice too.
Great park friendly helpful staff and great rates.
Amenities need more attention and rubbish removal.
An amazing caravan park we loved our overnight stay. Old but spotless amenities. Great camp kitchen and fire pits, a pool. Trees between each site for shade.
A beautiful caravan park, lovely grounds, very peaceful with nice facilities!
Great location and park but needs attention to cleaning and upkeep as it's the dearest park around

Hidden gem! This is the perfect base to explore the area. The caravan park is beautifully clean with top notch facilities - awesome camp kitchen, clean and tidy pool, spotless ablutions blocks and hot showers! Fire pits are dotted around the park with ash bins. I wish we could have stayed longer! For dog owners - there are two fully enclosed off lead areas in the memorial park, right next to the caravan park. Our pooch had a few fantastic off lead runs to burn off all that energy she stored on a long car journey. Must also mention how lovely the staff were! 10/10.
Had a great relaxing stay here, friendly staff and great cabin. Daughter loved the playground. Would definitely stay again.
Nice caravan park, big easy sites, recommend this place if your looking around the flinders rangers or a overnight stay if heading to lake eyre
Large, spacious, well maintained park. Had a fair bit of rain while we were here but the water drained away well. Team members were lovely. Would definitely come back 🥰

This was part of our stop on the way to Marree where we were to catch a flight over Lake Eyre. We have stayed at this park previously but thought it had been tidied up a bit from last time here. The service at reception was fantastic and I had a couple of requests that were easily met. The amenities while older are clean and well maintained.

The cabins aren't the most modern, but is that why people come to Hawker in the middle of the Flinders? We loved our stay here. The manager is super friendly, he changed our cabin so we could have bunk beds without any hassle, the cabin was quite large, the bed was good... There's a swimming pool for children... And the location is great, about 20 minutes from the first nice hikes in the area (although the one 10 minutes from the campground in Jarvis is already cool). Honestly, it's great for anyone who wants to come camping in the Flinders.

Good stop to visit the Flinders Ranges. We had an ensuite site was great.
Pool was nice, no signup that dogs arent allowed & unfortunately some guests took their dogs for a swim 🫤

Omg these guys are fantastic. They have looked after us. Very much appreciated for everything🥂🎉
Happy New Year

Hi readers, hawker is the starting town while heading to Flinder Ranges. This is a small town with basic needs; particularly we liked this caravan park which is with cabins, unpowered and powered sites, caravan sites, communal space for bbq, laundry, swimming pool, flush toilets and showers. This is pet friendly too. Moreover, this town is worth for talking tour of this by foot. You will explore small park, memorial park,  worth reading history, general store, Jeff’s Art Gallary,  pub and food restaurant. We have had an amazing stay here. Thanks
Beautiful peaceful park. Lots of trees, grass camping areas, amazing staff, spotless amenities, base to shoot off from to see all the amazing sightseeing gems
Older park. Facilties also, for most part servicable.

​Hawker Caravan Park is an absolute fantastic gem, and we thoroughly enjoyed every moment of our stay! If you’re looking for the perfect base to explore the Flinders Ranges, this is it.
​The standout feature of this park is, without a doubt, the current staff. They are truly amazing, helpful, and incredibly attentive to your needs. From a friendly and speedy check-in to offering genuinely useful local knowledge they go above and beyond to ensure everyone feels welcome and looked after. It’s rare to find such exceptional service in a caravan park.
​Beyond the wonderful people, the park itself is immaculate. The powered sites are well-maintained, and the amenities blocks are spotless. It offers a quiet, relaxed atmosphere, making it the perfect place to unwind after a big day of exploring.
​We highly recommend Hawker Caravan Park!! Thank you to the incredible team for making our stay so enjoyable!
Fantastic park to stay in all the amenities are acceptable clean and tidy. Friendly staff that are knowledgeable of local area and they are willing to help in any way. Well done!!!

Very tidy, very clean and very well run… staff very helpful… we had a broken down van… (don’t ask)… and we moved into cabin when it was towed

Close to everything you want in the area… more than happy to visit this place again

Thanks!

Great location, friendly staff. Nice clean park just a bit more expensive than other caravan parks. Recommend staying at Hawker and visit some of the local attractions like Geoff Morgan's gallery. We used this location to travel out each day to different destinations in the Flinders Ranges. Has most of what you need, groceries,  fuel, food and coffee.
Great location to use as base camp for Flinders Ranges. Friendly owner, clean park, close to town. Dog friendly highly recommend.

What a great caravan park. This park has everything you need clean and well maintained. Renovated cabins, good caravan sites and grassed areas for camping. The camp kitchen is great for entertaining bigger groups and even has a 📺.

Over ptoced. $45 uncovered. 1 ablution block
with 4 showers in both men and women's block down the far end of the park for about 60 sites. Tiles missing from floor. A dump will never stay there again
Good facilities and very friendly staff- certainly worth a visit
Lovely neat park, amenities clean and useable

Just spent 4 great nights at this lovely park
5 caravans on powered sites with terrific lawned areas for group socialising and get together excellent camp kitchen and amenities perfect location to venture into wilpena for the day and jump into the beautiful pool to freshen up
Could not recommend highly enough just loved our 4 nights
Great base camp for exploring the Flinders. Good pool. Nice an quiet around the park. Cottage could of been a little cleaner. But other then that it was great.
This is the standard that all other parks in Australia should inspire too.

Spent 3  very enjoyable nights here, an ideal place to explore the fabulous Flinders Ranges.  Kathy and staff were very helpful 👏🏼 👍 and made our stay so pleasant. Great to bring our dog as well. Fabulous fenced dig park close by so 🐕 was super happy too
Many thanks 😊

Great powered sites. Tent pegs will go in the ground with a good hammer, but a drill would make things easier. This is expected for the Flinders Ranges. Reasonably priced clean laundry. Free camp kitchen. Clean bathrooms. The view is beautiful and it's quiet. The staff were really helpful and had great local knowledge. We stayed twice.
Great park easy to fit our 21ft van in and what fantastic service 👏 would be happy to drop in anytime 😊
The managers gave us 2 sites so we had a good drive through site to stay hitched up. Only charged for one site. Clean amenities. All good.

Super location for access to the Flinders Ranges. A well laid out park with everything you'd expect, however operators could do with being a bit more 'house-proud'.

Small thigs let them down such as no soap in either ladies bathrooms for the entire 3 days we were there (didn't appear to have been cleaned either) even after reporting this to reception. The BBQ camp kitchen was great, however the second more enclosed camp kitchen had a solid layer of dust over everything - like it was completely forgotten, in addition when we tried to use the oven, it was broken. The oven is a minor detail, but hygiene matters should be taken seriously and soap is a MUST - they took out the garbage everyday, why not check the soap?

Great stop over.
Friendly and accommodating staff.
Clean facilities
Please we stopped over.

Extremely helpful at 9pm after a lot of us were evacuated from wilpena due to wild weather. Nice park, clean, good playground for kids. Also recommend folks visit the Jeff Morgan gallery in hawker and pay for the self guided tour. An unexpected gem!

Just a quick overnight stay on a powered site $55 p night for 2. Drive through sites available. Nice shady sites. Old amenities block that ran out of paper during our stay and could certainly do with a freshen up. But clean enough.  Pick your own site if no one there and go to the office to pay and provide site number. Grounds clean and location very quiet. Would stay again.
We enjoyed everything about our 3 day stay.

During reception and check in we were provided with touring information, places of interest, maps , suggestions, lots of ideas on what to do amd see.   It was really amazing.  And we took on board all the suggestions and had amazing few days exploring the area. We went places we would not have known to go and they were awe inspiring and we are so thankful that she took the time to show us maps and make suggestions ... she definitely went over and above . And what a lovely, quiet, secluded , clean caravan park !  Loved it.

Over priced, $49.00 for unpowered site.
Set up of amenities not user friendly.
Amenities are old and airy.
Plenty of empty spaces, I wonder why, lol, next time we will drive back to Quorn caravan park
Pretty good place to stay. Very central to check out the flinders Ranges. Would recommend.

Clean amenities.
Good well stocked camp kitchen.
Ensuite sites and cabins available.
Helpful friendly staff.
They are fantastic we were able to upgrade from a powered site to an ensuite powered site which we are far more comfortable with.

A nice park for an arid area. A bit overpriced compared to other parks we have stayed in.
If I didn’t have to stop to get new tyres I would have kept going, or stayed a day longer at Rawnsley.

You’re better off camping on the side of the road!  We were in the overflow -supposedly a powered site!  It’s currently a dust bowl, unstable ground, no water and no amenities at all. Power went off about 8.30pm (all 6 sites around us) - a complaint at the office was useless. They clearly know about the issue but won’t/can’t do anything about it. Ablution blocks poor - no basin in one toilet and hand sanitizer empty.

I previously posted that i thought it the nightly rate was overpriced. Unfortunately, my math skills let me down. My sincere apologies to Ben and staff. Nice caravan park, amenities dated but spotless. Great base to explore the Flinders . Ben and the staff are amazing.

Stayed overnight in a motorhome with car and found the facilities great for a brief stay. We were running a bit late and the office accommodated us with special arrangements . A lovely park like rural setting and great for our dogs too.

Much of what people say in their reviews of Hawker Caravan Park is correct, in my experience. So I'll focus on two issues. First, the cost. Not only is 50 AUD substantially more expensive than in 2024 for a 'tent' site. Half the cost of a hotel room for a patch of bare dirt and access to communal bathrooms and fresh water? Second, the internet. I was assured at check-in that new investments make the internet accessible to all parts of the park. It isn't available even in communal areas like the kitchens. What is the point of an internet service that no one can use?
$49 for an unpowered site? What a joke

Nice tidy park, simple facilities that cover what you need. It has a small pool. The pitches are mostly level and had enough room to fit my van, awning and car on the pitch without being too squashy. The ensuites are compact but clean, and have great showers, small sinks and a toilet.
As a location for the Flinders it's probably about the closest to Adelaide, though being located at the southern end may make days trips into the Flinders longer each day.
Being in a town there are good facilities like food, fuel and mobile phone service.
Keep an eye out for the Sturt Desert peas!

Well presented caravan park, very CLEAN but simple amenities block,  Pet friendly. |The current managers are very welcoming, the park has plenty of powered & unpowered sites, 13 cosy cabins and within walking distance to centre of Hawker, highly recommend a stay.

I rolled up way after dark hoping to grab a spot, but found with the Hawker Races on it was chock'a'block full. The lady was extremely helpful & patient telling me about other options I could use in both Hawker & Quorn. Thank you .||Review must have a minimum of 200 characters - done.

Overly-expensive [almost $60 a night for small trailer van] given that ALL the clothes driers are out of order. Bathrooms are dirty (yes, it may be dusty throughout, and that is fine given the location. But, the cobwebs and grubbiness are not acceptable).
Awsome caravan Park. Great custom service great facilities
We're camping here for a week. Lovely large sites. Very helpful and friendly owner. This is not a motel, but they do have lots of cabins .
Overnight stay on the BikeSA Cycle Odyssey. Facilities great and close to town.
just Fabulous

We broke down in Hawker and ended up staying at this great caravan park for nearly a week. We were very comfortable there  and Kathy and hubby who run the park are fantastic! Friendly and kind people who were a pleasure to stay with. Water is very drinkable which was a surprise in such a remote location. Highly recommend this park when visiting the Flinders Ranges area. 🤩
Beautiful location at Hawker. Such a beautiful park! So clean and tidy…there’s a lot of love gone into making it special! Loved all the gumtrees!
Beautiful place !!

Great park to go too before heading north to Flinders Ranges.
Great sites, clean amenities.
Pool was great too.
Freindly staff.
Highly recommend.
We also went back to the park after the Flinders Ranges 👍
Great Caravan Park, Beautiful pool. Staff very friendly.
See under Noteworthy Experience
We and our friends had drive through sites. The staff were friendly. The amenities were adequate. Hawker is a good place to go south to Quorn and north to Wilpena Pound and further.
Great managers. They have cabins as well as drive though van parking. Great place to use as a home base for visiting the Flinders. This is not a hotel but a caravan park.
Very friendly helpful staff.
Great facilities. Service excellent plus. We left our computer power cable there and not only did they ring and let us know but Cathy also posted it back to us. Over and above service. Thank you :)

Practically last sizeable stop before the outback, except Leigh Creek. The caravan park is definitely showing its age and lack of care. The toilet and shower blocks appear to have no updates since the 70s. They don't get a lot of cleaning either.
Water is hot, but that's the only positive. Paper wrappers in the shower stall were there long enough to have cobwebs on them, and at least four days that we were there.
At $45 pn, I would expect a lot more cleanliness.
Too expensive even without power would bypass next time

Hawker Caravan Park is a very friendly and welcoming place to stay on your travels, offering spacious powered and unpowered sites along with comfortable and clean cabins. They were able to accommodate us with last minute bookings on two occasions due to unfortunate events with the van for which we cannot thank them enough.  We look forward to staying there again when next passing through Hawker.
Had an unpowered site. Great spot, clean amenities. Quiet. Cathy at reception was very helpful.

Great country accommodation. The cabin was clean and the beds super comfortable. The park was very busy as in peak tourist season, and the park is a quiet with respectful, pleasant campers. Lovely front of house office staff.

Was very busy on the day we were there. Think everyone was avoiding the rain. The site we were allocated was small, Fortunately there was an area next to us to park up the truck after we unhitched. Amenities were clean. Have stayed here before and would stay again. Reasonable value at $45 for power and water.
Great caravan park with all amenities, close to the pub and the gateway to  all the Flinders Ranges has to offer.
Great place to stay. One thing though, We couldnt find hot water in the camp kitchen to do the dishes. Will visit again. Lovely people to deal with
Great camping on the eastern side of Hawker township.

We stayed in the end Cottage which was very comfortable and had all the things one needed after sightseeing for the day or just on business.
I know the park has just had a few new cottages installed and has cabins and provides camping space. The only thing that isn't really here is a decent view once in the park, and not a lot of privacy, the cottage being the exception.

Great place to stay whilst in Hawker. The cabin has been ‘refreshed’ and they were clean and welcoming after a long day on the road.  Close to all amenities - the only criticism we had, is  the blind for the window which overlooks the park, has no sheer blind to offer privacy.  The cabin was dark and it would have been nice to open the blind without compromising privacy.    Just a sheer roller blind is all it needs.  Thank you.

Great location and very friendly service. Also good wifi. The cabins are clean with plenty of bedding but are small and dated and more like a motel room. There are not enough power points,  and a kitchenette rather than a kitchen....plan to BBQ or eat out. With 2 adults and 3 teenagers, there just wasn't enough room to fit comfortably. One positive was everyone was in bed by 9.30!
Enjoyed our stay with our little dog, staff very friendly. Great base with a big van to do the Flinders Ranges
Great little park and perfect for a stopover travelling to or from the ranges.
Awesome Caravan Park, with lovely shady tent site and the best swimming pool after a hot day driving. Highly recommend for a overnight stay before the Flinders Ranges.

Excellent caravan park. Very clean and tidy.
Managers are most accommodating. Love coming to this area.
Has a swimming pool
Very friendly couple working here. All facilities were very clean and the pool is great to cool off.

We enjoyed a quiet night here on an unpowered site,  with the layout did need to unhook.  Great bush setting with nice outlook to the Flinders Ranges.  Beautiful town with a couple of great looking pubs.  Agreed with another comment about being a preferred park as Parachilna and Leigh Creek didnt look as inviting for a stopover.  Lots of groundswork being done here to make a nice experience for travellers.  Appreciated, thank you!
Cabins are basic but clean. Great starting point into the Flinders for day drives. Unfortunately the pool was still cold in 30 degree weather. Would recommend for a few nights.

We were going to stay one night but ended up staying a few more. The owner and his dad are super lovely and very accommodating, the park has a real lovely feel about it, it’s honestly one of our favorite CP. Amenities were super clean, shower was great and pool was a treat. It’s a great spot to explore the area and has a fantastic public dog park across the road (a few bindi’s there atm).
We came after 5 and the office was shut there’s a sign saying find a spot and pay in the morning, which was a relief. Short walk Into town and great views of the FR. Didn’t feel cramped or crowded and it was easy to maneuver out 20’ CV.
Thanks Ben. See you on the way back!

We have been here one night, 3 to go, and have already come to the conclusion that this is one of the best caravan parks we have been to. The staff have been so welcoming and helpful. The location is very suitable for exploring the Flinders Ranges and Hawker provides many options for eating and shopping.
Nice place and quiet
Thank you, we will stay again!

Staff was really helpful. We had a situation where our car met with an accident and was waiting for a tow service which delayed our checkout time for couple of hours. It was so kind of the staff, she offered us to get dropped in nearby transportation service to Adelaide and not charging us for couple of hours of extending our stay because of this accident.

Best service and friendly people.

The staff was so helpful guiding us and allowing us that little extra time because of an car accident happened to us the previous night. I wasn't able to go around much but surely coming back again. 😊

The owners could not have been more obliging or helpful.
Due to heat and low rainfall it is dusty but the spaces are large and ours was shady.
The pool was sparkling clean and while the amenities aren't new the too were clean.
They have done a lot of planting and in a couple of years it will be even better.

Great vibe! Spacious! Just a generally chill place to stay. Would stay again 100%. Easy to book online. Playground and swimming pool. Communal fire. Did not get placed right next to another person which was nice to have some space. Loved it.
Although a bit dusty it is well situated for visiting the Flinders Ranges and Wilpena Pound. Friendly staff.
As a gateway to the many parts of The Flinders the park could do with some updates. The amenities in particular are very old.
Good honest caravan park with close access to the flinders ranges.

The owners/ employees are very encompassing and care about their service. I organised to store my car here while I walked the heysen trail, I arrived late and they kindly gave me a lift in order to help me. I didn't stay here, but the park is clean and free of rubbish and the gardens are kept well.
Loved everything about it, the views & setting😍
Nice clean and quiet place, good location to get out and enjoy the surrounds
never been there but I know it's busy so must be OK.
We stayed overnight and enjoyed the facilities.
